title: Why India is spending Rs 52,000 crore on the first 100 kilometres of the battlefield
content: India's defence acquisitions are shifting focus from expensive big-ticket items to weapons crucial for the initial hours of conflict. Recent approvals prioritize anti-drone systems, short-range air defence, and kamikaze drones, reflecting lessons from Operation Sindoor and preparing for a two-front contingency. This move signifies a move towards network-centric warfare, emphasizing tactical edge and survivability.

title: Bethell breaks Indian hearts on Sooryavanshi's debut; England take 1-0 lead
content: Bethell's unbeaten 76 off 46 balls helped England chase down 191 with an over to spare, giving the hosts a four-wicket win in the second match of the five-game series on Saturday.

title: Operation Entebbe: How Israel’s 1976 hostage rescue unfolded
content: In a remarkable display of tactical prowess, Israeli commandos executed Operation Thunderbolt under the cover of night at Entebbe Airport, successfully liberating over 100 hostages from terrorists. This high-stakes rescue unfolded after failed diplomatic negotiations, leading to a fierce debate over national sovereignty. The release of previously classified documents sheds light on the contentious discussions that paved the way for this audacious military intervention.

title: After 8,000 layoffs, Meta CEO Zuckerberg admits AI progress was slower than expected
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg acknowledged internal restructuring hasn't yielded expected results, particularly in AI agent development, admitting executives 'miscalculated' timelines.  Despite significant layoffs and AI investments, progress has been slower than anticipated.  However, Zuckerberg anticipates substantial AI benefits within three to six months.  Separately, the company addressed mouse-tracking software, assuring no employee data was used for AI training and confirming future opt-in policies.

title: ‘5-star conman’, who stayed at 300 luxury hotels across India without paying, held
content: A 69-year-old man, inspired by Charles Sobhraj, has been arrested after allegedly defrauding a Raipur hotel of over Rs 63,000 and stealing a laptop. For 36 years, he's been living a life of luxury in high-end hotels across India, often using false identities, before disappearing without paying. Police are investigating his claims of targeting nearly 300 hotels.

title: India eyes $1 trillion exports milestone: Which sectors will drive growth?
content: During the April-June quarter, exports saw a 15% growth in merchandise shipments and around 11% in services exports. Which sectors have contributed to exports growth? What role has the PLI scheme played and will trade deals help expedite growth? We decode:

title: Bengaluru horror: Woman lured to 'belated birthday' party, raped; five arrested
content: Five men have been arrested in Bengaluru after a woman was allegedly raped at a party. She was invited to a belated birthday celebration by an acquaintance, Somashekhar, who then took her to his house with friends. After consuming a drink, she became drowsy and was allegedly assaulted by one of the men while semi-conscious. Police have identified and apprehended all five accused.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.

title: Couple expected to photograph a moose, then a calf and bear burst out
content: A peaceful fishing trip in Maine took a dramatic turn when Todd and Elvia Malcolm witnessed a black bear pursuing a moose calf. Reacting swiftly, Todd drove his truck between the predator and its prey, startling the bear and allowing the calf to escape to its mother. Elvia, meanwhile, captured the heart-pounding moments on her phone, unknowingly documenting the incredible rescue

title: Billion-year-old hidden magma system on Mars may have supported life
content: Scientists have uncovered evidence of a massive magma system beneath Mars' surface, challenging the long-held belief that such complex geological activity requires tectonic plates. NASA's InSight lander data revealed a distinct boundary deep within the Martian crust, suggesting a vast, ancient magma chamber that cooled and crystallized. This discovery hints that the ingredients for life might be more common on Mars than previously thought, prompting a re-evaluation of planetary habitability.

title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to join Elon Musk, Sundar Pichai in space mission
content: Nvidia is ramping up its space computing ambitions with its first orbital data center, Space-1. The tech giant is actively hiring for a System Software Principal Architect to develop software for this new platform, designed to withstand harsh space conditions. This move signals Nvidia's commitment to orbital AI infrastructure, joining other tech giants like SpaceX and Google in exploring space-based connectivity and data centers.

title: Security forces launch major anti-terror operation in J&K's Shopian
content: On Saturday, security forces launched a substantial anti-terror operation in the Meemandar area of Shopian, triggered by credible intelligence regarding militant activity. The team is currently conducting an exhaustive cordon and search process, diligently exploring the region. According to officials, this operation began based on targeted inputs, and more information will be revealed as the search progresses.

title: Unwanted record! Sooryavanshi becomes first Indian batter in T20I history to ...
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi made history as India's youngest international cricketer during his T20I debut against England. While he showcased a fearless 14-run cameo with two sixes, he also became the first Indian to be stumped on debut.  Despite the early dismissal, Sooryavanshi's entry into the record books at 15 years and 99 days marks a significant milestone, surpassing Sachin Tendulkar's long-held record.

title: Doctor, engineer… or something else? How India’s career dream is changing
content: In India, engineering and medicine have long been the cornerstones of middle-class aspirations, symbolizing security and respect. Yet, the landscape is transforming. With the rise of AI and digital sectors, the job market now prizes flexibility and contemporary skills over old-fashioned degrees. Professions in AI, cybersecurity, and digital marketing are emerging as attractive, rewarding alternatives to traditional paths, proving that the definition of success has evolved.

title: China threat sparks alarm: Taiwan launches biggest reserve military overhaul in decades
content: Taiwan is significantly boosting its military reserve training, doubling mandatory service to 14 days and incorporating modern weaponry like drones and Himars. This overhaul addresses a critical troop shortage driven by a declining birth rate and escalating pressure from China. The reforms aim to equip reservists with skills for contemporary warfare, drawing lessons from the Ukraine conflict, as the island fortifies its defense capabilities.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's debut: Two sixes, 10 balls and a record-breaking day
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi made history on Saturday, becoming India's youngest international cricketer at 15 years and 99 days during the second T20I against England. The left-handed batter impressed with a quickfire 14 off 10 balls, including two sixes, before being stumped. His aggressive start, featuring a massive hit off Jofra Archer, showcased his immense potential despite the brief innings.

title: Mex some noise: England delay Mexico arrival fearing 'spies', greeted by boos
content: According to The Sun, England manager Thomas Tuchel and the Football Association chose to keep the squad at their training base in Kansas City for as long as possible before travelling to Mexico City. The move was made to prevent rival teams from watching England's training sessions before the knockout match.

title: Siya Goyal’s father says viral 'middle finger' moment was caused by car door accident
content: The father of Siya Goyal, accused in the Ketan Agarwal murder case, has refuted claims that his daughter made an obscene gesture while in police custody. He stated that her injured fingers were a result of an accident involving a car door during a legal formality at their residence. A video circulating online had shown Goyal appearing to make the gesture, sparking public outcry.

title: India-UK trade pact: Centre notifies rules for determination of origin of goods
content: India's Finance Ministry has finalized rules for goods eligible for benefits under the India-UK trade pact, set to begin July 15. This Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) will grant duty-free access to 99% of Indian exports, boosting sectors like textiles and engineering. Exporters must now provide a certificate of origin to claim these tariff advantages, ensuring the pact's integrity and preventing misuse.

title: 'Criticism is welcome, rumours are not': Puri on E20 fuel buzz, engine damage claims
content: Union Minister Hardeep Singh Puri has addressed social media concerns regarding ethanol-blended fuel, dismissing claims of pest attraction and engine damage. He emphasized that ethanol usage is a century-old concept and highlighted past government efforts. Recent tests by ARAI and OEMs indicate a minor drop in fuel efficiency with E20, while Toyota clarified a specific vehicle issue was due to fuel contamination, not ethanol.

title: 'How long will govt ignore our voices': Dipke writes to PM Modi as protest enters Day 15
content: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has written to Prime Minister Narendra Modi, demanding he address education activist Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike and the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged exam irregularities. The protest, now in its 15th day, highlights concerns over repeated exam leaks and student suicides, with Dipke criticizing the government's silence and alleged mistreatment of protesters.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: The incredible journey from Samastipur to Team India
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has etched his name in history, becoming the youngest Indian cricketer to debut internationally, surpassing even Sachin Tendulkar's record. Hailing from a small Bihar village, his journey is a testament to immense talent and dedication, marked by early successes in domestic and age-group cricket, and a record-breaking IPL career.  His father's unwavering support fueled this remarkable ascent, culminating in a dream come true on the international stage.

title: 'May next 250 years bring greater prosperity': PM Modi congratulates Trump on US Independence Day
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi extended warm wishes to US President Donald Trump and the American people on their 250th Independence Day. He emphasized the shared democratic values and the strength of the India-US partnership, expressing optimism for its future growth and prosperity. The US marked this significant milestone with nationwide festivities, celebrating its founding and democratic ideals.

title: Is E20 fuel safe for your vehicle? Here's what experts say
content: Concerns surrounding E20 ethanol-blended petrol are being addressed by industry experts, who assure its safety for all vehicles, including older models. Years of rigorous scientific testing and consultations with automakers have confirmed its efficacy. This nationwide rollout aims to significantly reduce India's reliance on crude oil imports, offering a more sustainable and secure energy future while maintaining vehicle performance and environmental benefits.

title: Why India is planning Namo Cities—and what they could mean for Delhi-NCR
content: India is embarking on a massive urban transformation, planning to build new cities around high-speed transit to accommodate a projected population surge in the National Capital Region. These "Namo Cities," part of Regional Plan-2041, will be developed as Transit-Oriented Developments, integrating housing, jobs, and services around Namo Bharat RRTS stations.

title: 'Academic jihad': BJP seeks ban on J&K textbook 'glorifying terrorists'; LG suspends 8
content: The Lieutenant Governor of Jammu and Kashmir has taken decisive action by suspending officials and initiating an inquiry into school library books deemed to contain 'inappropriate content.' This decision comes after reports surfaced of a book that seemingly glorified terrorists and separatists while promoting a harmful narrative. Consequently, authors and publishers face blacklisting, and the distribution of such publications is being halted across the Union Territory.

title: 'Strangled with dupatta': Delhi woman kills husband in front of mother-in-law
content: A shocking incident unfolded in east Delhi's Rashid Market where a 20-year-old woman allegedly strangled her 19-year-old husband to death. The crime, reportedly committed in a fit of rage, occurred after the husband accused her of an extramarital affair and confiscated her phone. Despite his mother's attempts to intervene, the woman allegedly used her dupatta to commit the act. The accused has been arrested.

title: Tilak Varma hands Sooryavanshi historic India debut cap - Watch
content: The 15-year-old received his maiden India cap from Tilak Varma before the toss and was all smiles as he prepared for his first international appearance.

title: Aamir, Gauri Home Wedding Plans: actor confirms July 5 ceremony; Pali Hill home glows in lights
content: Aamir Khan will marry Gauri Spratt on July 5 in a private ceremony at his Pali Hill home, now decorated with fairy lights. The couple will register their marriage under the Special Marriage Act, followed by lunch for close family and friends. Reconnected after 25 years, this marks Aamir's third marriage.

title: Historic! Sooryavanshi makes India debut, breaks Tendulkar's 36-year-old record
content: Teenage prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi etched his name in history by becoming India's youngest-ever international cricketer at 15. His remarkable journey, fueled by a stellar IPL 2026 season where he was the highest scorer and bagged multiple awards, saw him surpass Sachin Tendulkar's debut age record. Sooryavanshi's explosive talent was evident from his U-19 World Cup heroics to his IPL contract at 13 and a record-breaking century.

title: 'Not a single brick laid': MP's 100-bed hospital fully functional on 'paper' for 6 years
content: Although a 100-bed civil hospital in Indore has been operational on paper for six years, construction has yet to commence due to persistent land acquisition issues. Curiously, 87 positions have been filled, with staff currently working at alternative facilities. The government blames land scarcity, while opposition leaders are calling for a comprehensive investigation into this apparent administrative failure.

title: 'Wedding that would never happen': Siya Goyal's 'texts' to friend surface amid murder probe
content: A viral Snapchat screenshot allegedly shows Siya Goyal requesting Aadhaar card details for non-existent wedding tickets, days after she and Chetan Chaudhary were remanded to judicial custody for the murder of Pune realtor Ketan Agarwal. The couple is accused of pushing Agarwal, Goyal's fiancé, off a cliff. Police are examining coded chats and a second recovered mobile phone, with a key witness now coming forward.

title: 'Rain only good for those in balconies': Every monsoon, same old story in Mumbai
content: Mumbai's annual monsoon test has begun with the season's first heavy downpour, exposing civic unpreparedness. Tragic incidents, including a boy's death from a fallen tree and a man drowning in an open manhole, have marred the arrival of the rains. Commuters faced disruptions, with social media buzzing about flooded roads and delayed trains, highlighting the city's ongoing struggle with its monsoon challenges.

title: 'Became difficult to continue working': Chandrima Bhattacharya explains TMC exit
content: Chandrima Bhattacharya resigned from all Trinamool Congress posts, citing a loss of confidence from party chief Mamata Banerjee. Bhattacharya rejected claims of disloyalty, stating her decision stemmed from a phone call where Banerjee blamed her for the rebel faction's takeover of the party office. This move follows the TMC's internal crisis and a significant electoral defeat.

title: Why did Keir Starmer leave 10 Downing Street? Outgoing UK PM reveals the reason
content: Keir Starmer revealed his decision to step down as Labour leader and Prime Minister was a deeply personal one, made with his family at Chequers. He emphasized the need for his successor to navigate ongoing global and domestic challenges, defending his record of rebuilding the party. Starmer plans to remain an MP but will refrain from advising his successor, aiming to ensure the next government's success.

title: Pagdi system explained: Why is redeveloping these buildings so difficult?
content: Maharashtra's Pagdi system uniquely allows tenants to enjoy long-term occupancy by paying a one-time premium and then minimal rent. Although landlords keep ownership, tenants benefit from robust protections under the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999. The challenge arises when redeveloping older properties, often complicated by disputes. In response, the government has introduced a new framework to streamline this process, incorporating specialised FSI incentives and efficient dispute resolution to facilitate redevelopment.

title: Innovate and become job creators, Odisha Governor exhorts students
content: Update your knowledge in line with rapid technological advancements and explore opportunities in emerging fields such as AI, data science, robotics and quantum technology, advises Hari Babu at the Graduation Day ceremony at RVR & JC College of Engineering

title: As monsoon rains falter, Wayanad farmers in Kerala fear poor harvest
content: In Wayanad, rainfall deficit has adversely affected agriculture. The district received only 270.23 mm of rain in June, the lowest for the month in the past five years, according to Hume Centre for Ecology and Wildlife Biology

title: Mamata loyalist Chandrima Bhattacharya resigns as Bengal State president of Trinamool Congress
content: Her resignation is yet another setback for ex-CM Mamata Banerjee, who has only a handful of loyalists left in her camp; Ms. Bhattacharya also met Leader of Opposition Ritabrata Banerjee; she says once the ‘fundamental binding forces are gone, there is no reason for one to stay in the party or return to it’

title: Custodial death case: Sai Krishna’s mother pleads police for his mortal remains
content: Family members of the victim depose before the magisterial inquiry; mother alleges slow investigation

title: Assault case: T.N. Minister Marie Wilson told to appear before Puducherry court without fail on July 10
content: Mr. Wilson was supposed to appear before the court on Saturday to receive the additional charge sheet. However, his advocate informed the court of his inability to appear due to his Ministerial responsibilities

title: Minister inaugurates restored building of District Museum in Vellore fort complex
content: Prior to the restored building, the district museum was cramped with visitors due to limited space. Some artefacts were kept in the open, outside the building

title: Mamata hits out at Trinamool rebels, dares them to join BJP
content: The former West Bengal Chief Minister, broke her weeks-long silence when her party had split with a majority of MLAs and MPs deciding to switch their loyalties after the defeat in the Assembly polls; accuses the rebels of betraying the party after winning on the party symbol

title: Ram temple donation case: Congress questions RSS, asks why agencies are silent on ‘big thieves’
content: Uttar Pradesh Congress Legislature Party leader Aradhana Misra dismisses as ‘crocodile tears’ the statement issued by RSS general secretary Dattatreya Hosabale

title: TNPCB takes water samples from farm wells along Palar River in Tirupattur after complaints of froth
content: TNPCB officials said that the expert team will coordinate with the Tamil Nadu Water Supply and Drainage Board to ensure that residents get a permanent solution for froth formation in water bodies

title: India’s semiconductor push will create countless jobs: PM
content: Modi inaugurates semiconductor facility in Gujarat’s Sanand; says such clusters will bring about economic transformation; urges youth to tap the new opportunities in the emerging Artificial Intelligence era

title: Health activists seek revision of India’s essential medicines list to ensure price cap, availability
content: The Working Group on Access to Medicines and Treatments said NLEM was last notified in 2022 and contains 384 medicines, while the WHO Model List of Essential Medicines has since been revised in 2023 and again in 2025, and by the Working Group’s count now runs to 523 medicines

title: India weathered West Asia crisis through strategic decision-making, diplomacy: PM Modi
content: India avoided fuel rationing, LPG disruptions, price surges, with domestic refineries upping their LPG production and public sector OMCs bearing the losses, says PM; promises further expansion of refinery capacity

title: Late BJP MLA Vungzagin Valte laid to rest in Manipur four months after death
content: Manipur CM attends Valte’s last rites, who was on ventilator support for almost three years and later died in February this year; Khemchand Singh regrets not being by Valte’s side during 2023 attack; says ‘Valte’s family is like my family and his friends and close ones are my friends too’

title: Sleepless fans in Kerala put through wringer as Argentina goes to the brink and back
content: The close contest highlighted Argentina’s vulnerabilities, leaving fans across the state in a frenzy as they celebrated their team’s narrow escape from elimination.

title: Minister challenges YSRCP for debate on Rayalaseema development claims
content: Addressing the media, Janardhan Reddy says the YSRCP, which failed to establish a steel plant in Rayalaseema during its five-year tenure, is now attempting to steal the credit

title: Finance Ministry notifies rules for determination of origin of goods under India-U.K. trade pact
content: CETA secures duty-free access for 99% of India’s exports to the U.K., covering nearly the entire trade basket

title: Modern command control room opened in Dhone to boost public safety
content: A total of 118 CCTV cameras installed at key locations across the town have been integrated into the command control room

title: Former Ministers, Ex-MLAs of AIADMK who joined TVK meet Chief Minister Vijay in Panaiyur
content: TVK sources say this is the first time the new inductees have met Mr. Vijay, as all of them joined the party in the presence of Minister N. Anand

title: DMK bound together by ideology, will rise like a phoenix, says Stalin
content: The former Chief Minister asserts that no other political party could match the DMK’s organisational strength

title: U.S. DoJ says Adani case should never have been brought, urges judge to drop charges permanently
content: The filing came after U.S. District Judge Nicholas Garaufis asked the department to explain why it was seeking to permanently dismiss the indictment, calling its earlier motion “terse, bland, and conclusory”

title: More Chinese investment good for wider relationship, says India’s envoy to China
content: Noting that trade is expanding but lopsided, Ambassador Vikram Doraiswami pitches for more Indian exports to China, especially in areas such as pharmaceuticals where India is globally competitive

title: Kochi witnesses two-fold increase in NDPS cases in a month
content: Of the 1,143 drug-related cases registered in the first six months of the year, 503 were recorded in June alone following the launch of the new anti-drug operation

title: In A First, Ladakh Deploys 100 Ex-Armymen To Curb Eco Violations, Off-Roading
content: The move comes days after tourists were fined for driving through ecologically sensitive areas near Pangong Lake, as the administration steps up enforcement to protect the Union Territory's fragile environment.

title: Cannabis And A Corpse: UK Woman Arrested For Stabbing Boyfriend In Thailand
content: The police found a washed 50-centimetre machete was kept in a sink. The victim's girlfriend claimed that he had hurt himself; however, police found cuts on her fingertips and inconsistencies in her statement.

title: Trust Issues, Accusation: Chandrima Bhattacharya On Why She Quit Trinamool
content: Bhattacharya said Mamata Banerjee had accused her of "handing over" the Trinamool headquarters to the rebels.

title: Roads Flooded, Trains Delayed As Mumbai Get 200 mm Rain In 12 Hours
content: Torrential downpours battered Mumbai on Saturday, with several parts of the city receiving nearly 200 mm of rains in 12 hours, inundating low-lying areas, triggering road cave-ins, tree falls and house collapses, and disrupting road and rail traffic.

title: At Ali Khamenei's Funeral, Hezbollah And Hamas Members Meet Iran's Araghchi
content: Most foreign officials and dignitaries paid their respects on Friday to Khamenei, who ruled Iran from 1989 and was killed aged 86 along with several members of his family and top officials in a US-Israeli strike on February 28.

title: Heavy Rain In Pune Triggers Waterlogging, 2 Landslides
content: Pune witnessed heavy rains on Saturday, resulting in waterlogging on several roads as well as a couple of landslide incidents, though no one was hurt in these, officials said.

title: US Celebrates 250th Birthday As Trump Says American Identity "Under Attack"
content: US President Donald Trump has added a program of roaring military flyovers - and an unusual campaign-style political rally - for July Fourth.

title: "Kill Me To Stop Me": Defiant Mamata Banerjee Calls Trinamool Rebels "Traitors"
content: Mamata Banerjee said the Trinamool's symbol would remain with the bloc loyal to her, indicating that the rebels might face a protracted legal battle.

title: Rohit Lamba Gang Member Arrested For Attacking Rival's Aide In Delhi
content: Police said the attack was linked to an ongoing rivalry between the Rohit Lamba gang and the Mohit Bhardwaj alias Monu gang.

title: Palestinian Children Born From Smuggled Sperm Still Wait To Meet Freed Fathers
content: Several former detainees were deported to Egypt after their release, while their wives and children remain in the occupied West Bank, unable to travel because of Israeli restrictions.

title: Assam Outpaces National GST Growth Average In June
content: Assam has emerged among the top performing major states in the country in terms of monthly GST growth, reflecting improving economic activity and stronger revenue administration.

title: When A Birthday Reminds Of Unresolved Conflict: Beijing vs The Dalai Lama
content: Human rights organizations have pointed out increased surveillance, censorship, and a crackdown against public celebration of the Dalai Lama's birthday

title: "Case Against Adani Should Never Have Been Brought": US Justice Department
content: The United States pretending to be the world police can cause diplomatic strife and also wastes resources better spent on domestic concerns, said the DOJ.

title: J&K School Book Calls Terrorists "Great Personalities", Alleges Civil Group
content: The book, cleared under Samagra Shiksha, turns history on its head by celebrating anti-national figures as role models, the JKPF said

title: 'Get Valid Registration': J&K Lt Governor's Appeal To Amarnath Pilgrims
content: The administration reiterated that only those pilgrims possessing a valid registration would be permitted to proceed to the cave shrine.

title: Super Typhoon Bavi Approaches US Pacific Islands, Could Strike Monday
content: Bavi was moving westward with sustained winds of 269 kilometres per hour and gusts of 324 kph, the Joint Typhoon Warning Centre said in an update at 7:00 pm local time.

title: "Deeply Disturbing": Germany On Reports Of China Training Russian Troops
content: On May 20, the German daily Die Welt reported that the Chinese army had secretly trained several hundred Russian soldiers on its territory.

title: Anam calls for focus on entrepreneurial skills in academic curriculum
content: Addressing the second graduation day celebrations of PBR Vidyodaya Institute of Technology and Science, Minister urges the students to become entrepreneurs and job providers rather than job seekers

title: BMTC now accepts NCMC payments through new smart ticketing machines; adoption expected to rise
content: With the integration now in place, the NCMC issued by banks across India can be used on the city’s bus services, allowing thousands of daily commuters to use a single card for both metro and bus journeys

title: Adani-MSC deal: State govt. cautious as APSEZ clarifies stance in second letter
content: It remains unclear whether the mortgage rights over the port site and its assets granted to the AVPPL for the concession period will be applicable to the new partner, MSC. APSEZ tells State govt. that would MSC will not enjoy a monopoly at the port will be accessible to all users without discrimination

title: Ram Temple Trust meeting on July 6; SIT report, post of CEO part of agenda
content: Trust members are also likely to deliberate on the appointment of a Chief Executive Officer to oversee the management of the temple

title: A.P. govt. notifies vending zones in 51 Urban Local Bodies
content: Notified plans will provide clarity by identifying vending, regulated vending and no-vending zones, enabling vendors to carry out business without fear of arbitrary displacement, says MA&UD Minister

title: Andaman Lt. Governor holds meeting with MoEFCC Officials to discuss Great Nicobar project
content: In the meeting, it was discussed that during the construction and operational phases of mega strategic and infrastructure projects, mitigation measures must be implemented to eliminate environmental damage

title: Monsoon tracker July 4 LIVE: IMD issues red alert in five districts in Odisha
content: Incessant and heavy rains threw life out of gear in Mumbai and its metropolitan region; the monsoon is active in Gujarat with “exceptional heavy rainfall” in Junagadh, where a few areas witnessed severe waterlogging

title: ‘One Nation, One Election initiative would fracture country’s federal compact’
content: Conclave on ‘One-Nation One-Election, Federalism and Citizenship’ also raises concern on SIR, citizenship and ECI functioning

title: 'Ended the same war so many times': Biden Jr mocks Trump, names him for Peace Prize

title: USA@250: The United States of Anxiety - debt, divide and Donald Trump
content: the heat wave has turned parts of the celebration into an endurance test . some of his supporters attribute magical qualities to him . he promises an extra-long speech on the occasion of the country's 250th anniversary . the event was intended to showcase the nation's diversity through exhibits from 50 states .
